methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $7.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Ascend Elements
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Growth), post-money $3B+

data.narrative:
Stegra is a Growth-stage climate and energy company founded by Carl-Erik Lagercrantz and Harald Mix that has raised $7.0B+ to date. The relevant peer universe comprises 21 same-sector companies across public and private markets, including 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers such as Redwood Materials, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Helion, TAE Technologies, and Ascend Elements.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, as traditional peer-median comparison is not applicable. The comparable universe lacks sufficient narrow-filter matches (requiring ≥3 same-stage or adjacent-stage peers at similar valuation tiers); only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage peer met initial criteria, making sector-wide median analysis unreliable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier to the $7.0B total-raised proxy, yielding an implied value of $8.4B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $7.1B to $9.7B, with a midpoint of $8.4B. This range reflects limited triangulation depth: only one method yields robust output given sparse comparable data at this capital tier and stage. Key uncertainties include the true composition and performance of the private peer set, the applicability of the 1.2x multiplier to Stegra's specific development trajectory, and potential data gaps in the Climate & Energy sector at the Growth stage. Caution is warranted pending further market comparables or company-specific financial detail.
